# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Security group for EC2 instances with SSH, HTTP, and HTTPS access

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC ID where the security group will be created

Resources:
  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupName: ec2-web-ssh-security-group
      GroupDescription: Security group allowing SSH, HTTP, and HTTPS access from anywhere
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow SSH access from anywhere
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow HTTP access from anywhere
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow HTTPS access from anywhere
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow all outbound traffic
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EC2-Web-SSH-SecurityGroup
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Development

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: ID of the created security group
    Value: !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
    Export:
      Name: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-SecurityGroupId
  SecurityGroupName:
    Description: Name of the created security group
    Value: !GetAtt EC2SecurityGroup.GroupId
